bids.reasoning: The negotiation has just started (t=0.0). The opponent proposed an offer consisting entirely of `valueE`, which provides the absolute minimum utility for me across all issues. To establish a strong anchor and set clear expectations, I will start by proposing my ideal outcome: `valueA` for all issues, targeting my maximum possible utility of 1.00.

The negotiation is very early (t = 0.02). The opponent has offered their apparent maximum twice in a row, which is my absolute minimum (valueE across all issues). As a gesture of good faith to get the negotiation moving without giving up significant value, I will make a small concession on one of my least important issues (issueE) from my optimal preference to the next best, while holding firm on the highest-weighted issues (issueA, issueB, and issueC).

The current time is very early (t=0.04), and the opponent is completely holding firm on their opening offer of all 'valueE's, which provides me the lowest possible utility. I will continue to make small concessions on my least important issues (issueD and issueE) to encourage the opponent to move, while keeping my most heavily weighted issues (issueA, issueB, and issueC) at their maximum value. I will slightly adjust issueD down to 'valueB'.

The opponent is stubbornly repeating their initial offer, which provides me with the lowest possible utility (valueE on all issues). Since it is still very early in the negotiation (t=0.06), there is no need to make rushed concessions. I will continue making small concessions on my least important issues to show a willingness to negotiate, dropping my demand on issueE from valueB to valueC, while holding firm on my highest-weight issues (issueA, issueB, and issueC).

The opponent is starting to concede slightly on issueA, offering valueD instead of valueE. Given that we are still extremely early in the negotiation (t=0.08), I will continue to hold firm on my most important issues (issueA, issueB, and issueC) while making incremental concessions on my lower-weighted issues (issueD and issueE) to signal a willingness to compromise and uncover their priorities.

The current time fraction is 0.10, which is still very early in the negotiation, allowing me to maintain a very high utility threshold. The opponent has made a minor concession on issueA but is otherwise holding firm at their initial values. Because issueA and issueB are my most important issues, I will hold firm on them and concede slightly on issueE to signal a willingness to compromise without sacrificing much utility.

The time is still early (t=0.12). The opponent has barely started to explore different values on the most important issues (issueA and issueB), currently alternating between 'valueE' and 'valueD'. I will hold firm on my top priority issues (issueA and issueB) at 'valueA' to signal their importance, while making a small concession on my moderately important issue (issueC) to keep the negotiation moving forward and show flexibility.

The current time fraction is very early at 0.14, so I will continue to maintain a strong position, especially on my most important issues (issueA and issueB). The opponent is showing very little flexibility, largely proposing values that give me minimal utility. I am making a small concession on issueD to signal a willingness to negotiate while protecting my core utility priorities.

The time is still early (t=0.16), and the opponent has made very minor, probing concessions on issues A and B interchangeably, returning to their heavily favored positions. Since issues A and B are my most critical (0.3 weight each), I will hold firm on them. Instead, I will continue to incrementally concede on my lower-weighted issues (such as issueE), offering valueE to see if they might reciprocate.
